name: perlin-noise

metadata.name: perlin-noise
metadata.summary: Python implementation for Perlin Noise with unlimited coordinates space

documentation:
Smooth random noise generator  
read more https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perlin_noise  

source code: https://github.com/salaxieb/perlin_noise


noise = PerlinNoise(octaves=3.5, seed=777)  
     octaves : number of sub rectangles in each [0, 1] range  
     seed : specific seed with which you want to initialize random generator
     tile_sizes : tuple of ints of you want to noise seamlessly repeat itself  


```python
from perlin_noise import PerlinNoise


noise = PerlinNoise()
# accepts as argument float and/or list[float]
noise(0.5) == noise([0.5])
# --> True
# noise not limited in space dimension and seamless in any space size
noise([0.5, 0.5]) == noise([0.5, 0.5, 0, 0, 0])
# --> True
# noise can seamlessly repeat isself
noise([0.5, 0.5], tile_sizes=[2, 5]) == noise([2.5, 5.5], tile_sizes=[2, 5])
# --> True
```

Usage examples:
```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from perlin_noise import PerlinNoise

noise = PerlinNoise(octaves=10, seed=1)
xpix, ypix = 100, 100
pic = [[noise([i/xpix, j/ypix]) for j in range(xpix)] for i in range(ypix)]

plt.imshow(pic, cmap='gray')
plt.show()
```
![png](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/salaxieb/perlin_noise/master/pics/output_4_0.png)

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from perlin_noise import PerlinNoise

noise1 = PerlinNoise(octaves=3)
noise2 = PerlinNoise(octaves=6)
noise3 = PerlinNoise(octaves=12)
noise4 = PerlinNoise(octaves=24)

xpix, ypix = 100, 100
pic = []
for i in range(xpix):
    row = []
    for j in range(ypix):
        noise_val = noise1([i/xpix, j/ypix])
        noise_val += 0.5 * noise2([i/xpix, j/ypix])
        noise_val += 0.25 * noise3([i/xpix, j/ypix])
        noise_val += 0.125 * noise4([i/xpix, j/ypix])

        row.append(noise_val)
    pic.append(row)

plt.imshow(pic, cmap='gray')
plt.show()
```

![png](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/salaxieb/perlin_noise/master/pics/output_5_0.png)


Library has a possibility to generate repetative random noise with custom tile sizes:

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from perlin_noise import PerlinNoise

noise = PerlinNoise(octaves=2, seed=42)
xpix, ypix = 800, 1200
lim_x, lim_y = 6, 9
pic = [
    [
        noise([lim_x * i / xpix, lim_y * j / ypix], tile_sizes=[2, 3])
        for j in range(xpix)
    ]
    for i in range(ypix)
]

plt.imshow(pic, cmap="gray")
plt.show()
```

![png](https://github.com/salaxieb/perlin_noise/raw/master/pics/smooth_tiled.png)

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from perlin_noise import PerlinNoise

noise1 = PerlinNoise(octaves=1)
noise2 = PerlinNoise(octaves=3)
noise3 = PerlinNoise(octaves=6)
noise4 = PerlinNoise(octaves=12)

xpix, ypix = 800, 1200
lim_x, lim_y = 4, 6
tile_sizes = (2, 3)
pic = []
for i in range(ypix):
    row = []
    for j in range(xpix):
        noise_val = noise1([lim_x * i / xpix, lim_y * j / ypix], tile_sizes)
        noise_val += 0.5 * noise2([lim_x * i / xpix, lim_y * j / ypix], tile_sizes)
        noise_val += 0.25 * noise3([lim_x * i / xpix, lim_y * j / ypix], tile_sizes)
        noise_val += 0.125 * noise4([lim_x * i / xpix, lim_y * j / ypix], tile_sizes)

        row.append(noise_val)
    pic.append(row)

plt.imshow(pic, cmap="gray")
plt.savefig("pics/multy_noise_tiled.png", transparent=True)
plt.show()
```
![png](https://github.com/salaxieb/perlin_noise/raw/master/pics/multy_noise_tiled.png)

for tiles to work correctly, number of octaves **MUST** be integer
```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from perlin_noise import PerlinNoise

noise = PerlinNoise(octaves=2.5, seed=42)
xpix, ypix = 800, 1200
lim_x, lim_y = 6, 9
pic = [
    [
        noise([lim_x * i / xpix, lim_y * j / ypix], tile_sizes=(2, 3))
        for j in range(xpix)
    ]
    for i in range(ypix)
]

plt.imshow(pic, cmap="gray")
plt.savefig('pics/tiled_with_step.png', transparent=True)
plt.show()
```
![png](https://github.com/salaxieb/perlin_noise/raw/master/pics/tiled_with_step.png)

modules.name: perlin_noise
documentation: Perlin noise generator library.

modules.name: perlin_noise.tools
documentation: File for placing functions used in library.

functions.name: perlin_noise.tools.dot
documentation:
Two vectors dot product.

Parameters:
    vec1: List[float] - first vector
    vec2: List[float] - second vector

Returns:
    Dot product of 2 vectors

Raises:
    ValueError: if length not equal

functions.name: perlin_noise.tools.sample_vector
documentation:
Sample normalized vector given length.

Parameters:
    dimensions: int - space size
    seed: Optional[int] - random seed value

Returns:
    List[float] - normalized random vector of given size

functions.name: perlin_noise.tools.fade
documentation:
Smoothing [0, 1] values.

Parameters:
    given_value: float [0, 1] value for smoothing

Returns:
    smoothed [0, 1] value

Raises:
    ValueError: if input not in [-0.1, 1.1]

functions.name: perlin_noise.tools.hasher
documentation:
Hashes coordinates to integer number and use obtained number as seed.

Parameters:
    coordinates: Tuple[int, ...] - tuple of coordinates
    tile_sizes: Optional[Tuple[int, ...]] - optional tile sizes

Returns:
    hash of coordinates in integer

functions.name: perlin_noise.tools.product
documentation:
Multiplies values of iterable  each with each.

Parameters:
    iterable: - any iterable

Returns:
    product of values

modules.name: perlin_noise.rand_vec
documentation: Random vector generator used for weighting perlin noise.

classes.name: perlin_noise.rand_vec.RandVec

methods.name: perlin_noise.rand_vec.RandVec.__init__
documentation:
Vector initializer in specified coordinates.

Parameters:
    coordinates: Tuple[int] - vector coordinates
    seed: int - random init seed

methods.name: perlin_noise.rand_vec.RandVec.dists_to
documentation:
Calculate distance to given coordinates.

Parameters:
    coordinates: Tuplie[int] - coordinates to calculate distance

Returns:
    distance

methods.name: perlin_noise.rand_vec.RandVec.weight_to
documentation:
Calculate this vector weights to given coordinates.

Parameters:
    coordinates: Tuple[int] - target coordinates

Returns:
    weight

methods.name: perlin_noise.rand_vec.RandVec.get_weighted_val
documentation:
Calculate weighted contribution of this vec to final result.

Parameters:
    coordinates: calculate weighted relative to this coordinates

Returns:
    weighted contribution
Vectors to give weights and contribute in final value.

modules.name: perlin_noise.perlin_noise
documentation: Perlin Noise calculating lib.

classes.name: perlin_noise.perlin_noise.PerlinNoise

methods.name: perlin_noise.perlin_noise.PerlinNoise.__init__
documentation:
Perlin Noise object initialization class.

    ex.: noise = PerlinNoise(n_dims=2, octaves=3.5, seed=777)

Parameters:
    octaves : optional positive float, default = 1
        positive number of sub rectangles in each [0, 1] range
    seed : optional positive int, default = None
        specified seed

Raises:
    ValueError: if seed is negative

methods.name: perlin_noise.perlin_noise.PerlinNoise.__call__
documentation:
Forward request to noise function.

Parameters:
    coordinates: float or list of coordinates
    tile_sizes: optional tile sizes to repetative patterns

Returns:
    noise_value

methods.name: perlin_noise.perlin_noise.PerlinNoise.noise
documentation:
Get perlin noise value for given coordinates.

Parameters:
    coordinates: float or list of coordinates
    tile_sizes: optional tile sizes to repetative patterns

Returns:
    noise_value

Raises:
    TypeError: if coordinates is not valid type
    ValueError: if tile_sizes have different length than coordinates

methods.name: perlin_noise.perlin_noise.PerlinNoise.get_from_cache_of_create_new
documentation:
Use cached RandVec or creates new.

Parameters:
    coors: Tuple of int vector coordinates
    tile_sizes: optional tile sizes to repetative patterns

Returns:
    RandVec

Smooth random noise generator.

read more https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perlin_noise
